Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.provers.predicate

Class PredicateProver

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.provers.predicate.PredicateProver
All Implemented Interfaces:
IProverModule
public class PredicateProver extends Object implements IProverModule
This prover module is responsible for applying the unit resolution rule.

Each call to addClauseAndDetectContradiction(Clause) tries to detect a contradiction using all unit clauses. Each call to next(boolean) then returns one clause that is issued from the match between a unit and a non-unit clause.

    • next

      public ProverResult next(boolean force)
      Description copied from interface: IProverModule
      Returns the next prover result or ProverResult.EMPTY_RESULT if nothing can be inferred.

      A prover module can return ProverResult.EMPTY_RESULT even if something can be inferred. It happens when the prover module blocks itself, to allow other modules to be called. However, if force is true the module must return a result if it has one.

      TODO the module blocking mechanism should better go outside each module in the ClauseDispatcher, since it can control better what module should be called next.

      Specified by:
      next in interface IProverModule
      Parameters:
      force - true to enforce this module to return a non-empty result
      Returns:
      the result, and never null

    • nextUnit

      private Clause nextUnit()

    • addClauseAndDetectContradiction

      public ProverResult addClauseAndDetectContradiction(Clause clause)
      Description copied from interface: IProverModule
      Adds the specified clause to this module and tries to derive a contradiction from it. All clauses inferred while trying to derive a contradiction are usually returned by this method. If a contradiction is found, the FalseClause corresponding to the contradiction must be contained in the result.
      Specified by:
      addClauseAndDetectContradiction in interface IProverModule
      Parameters:
      clause - the clause to be added
      Returns:
      the clauses derived when trying to detect a contradiction, never null

    • removeClause

      public void removeClause(Clause clause)
      Description copied from interface: IProverModule
      Removes the clause from this module.

      TODO perhaps let this method return a ProverResult

      Specified by:
      removeClause in interface IProverModule
      Parameters:
      clause - the clause to remove

    • hadConditions

      private boolean hadConditions(Clause clause)

    • contradiction

      public ProverResult contradiction(Level oldLevel, Level newLevel, Set<Level> dependencies)
      Description copied from interface: IProverModule
      Lets this prover module update its data structure from the old to the new level. The result contains clauses derived by this prover module and by construction it cannot contain any TRUE or FALSE clauses.

      When this method is called, ILevelController.getCurrentLevel() returns the same value as the one in newLevel, i.e. newLevel.equals(levelController.getCurrentLevel()) == true. This holds until ILevelController.nextLevel() is called.

      Specified by:
      contradiction in interface IProverModule
      Parameters:
      oldLevel - the level before the contradiction has been derived
      newLevel - the level after the contradiction has been derived
      dependencies - the level dependencies of the contradiction
      Returns:
      the result, can never be null

    • registerDumper

      public void registerDumper(Dumper dumper)
      Description copied from interface: IProverModule
      Lets this prover module register the data structure it wishes be dumped.
      Specified by:
      registerDumper in interface IProverModule
      Parameters:
      dumper - the dumper on which the data structures can be registered
